---
title: "Building a Model"
author: "Simon Frost"
format:
  html:
    code-fold: false
    toc: true
  gfm:
    toc: true
engine: julia
---

## Overview

This vignette shows how to build Starsim.jl simulations from components:
People, Networks, Diseases, and parameters. We cover dict-based vs
component-based setup, heterogeneous contacts, and result export.

## Component-based setup

Each simulation component is a Julia struct. Pass them directly to `Sim`:

```{julia}
using Starsim
using Plots

# Create components explicitly
net = RandomNet(n_contacts=10)
disease = SIR(beta=0.1, dur_inf=4.0, init_prev=0.02)

sim = Sim(
    n_agents = 1_000,
    networks = net,
    diseases = disease,
    dt = 1.0,
    stop = 200.0,
    rand_seed = 42,
    verbose = 0,
)
run!(sim)
```

## Multiple networks

You can pass a vector of networks. Each gets a unique name:

```{julia}
sim_multi = Sim(
    n_agents = 1_000,
    networks = [
        RandomNet(name=:household, n_contacts=4),
        RandomNet(name=:workplace, n_contacts=8),
    ],
    diseases = SIR(beta=0.05, dur_inf=4.0, init_prev=0.01),
    dt = 1.0,
    stop = 200.0,
    rand_seed = 42,
    verbose = 0,
)
run!(sim_multi)

prev = get_result(sim_multi, :sir, :prevalence)
println("Peak prevalence with 2 networks: $(round(maximum(prev), digits=4))")
```

## Controlling time

The `dt` parameter sets the timestep. With `dt=1`, each step is 1 day.

```{julia}
# Compare dt=1 (daily) vs dt=0.5 (twice daily)
sim1 = Sim(n_agents=1000, networks=RandomNet(n_contacts=10),
    diseases=SIR(beta=0.05, dur_inf=4.0, init_prev=0.02),
    dt=1.0, stop=200.0, rand_seed=42, verbose=0)
run!(sim1)

sim2 = Sim(n_agents=1000, networks=RandomNet(n_contacts=10),
    diseases=SIR(beta=0.05, dur_inf=4.0, init_prev=0.02),
    dt=0.5, stop=200.0, rand_seed=42, verbose=0)
run!(sim2)

p1 = get_result(sim1, :sir, :prevalence)
p2 = get_result(sim2, :sir, :prevalence)
println("dt=1.0 peak: $(round(maximum(p1), digits=4))")
println("dt=0.5 peak: $(round(maximum(p2), digits=4))")
```

## Accessing and exporting results

```{julia}
using DataFrames

df = to_dataframe(sim)
println("Available columns: ", names(df))
first(df, 5)
```

## Saving and loading

Simulations can be serialized with Julia's `Serialization` stdlib:

```{julia}
using Serialization

# Save
io = IOBuffer()
serialize(io, sim.pars)
println("SimPars serialized: $(position(io)) bytes")
```
